recognized_artifact: Differential diagnosis procedure (clinical or analogous domain): a presenting input is decomposed into multiple candidate paths, a differentiating input is introduced to re-sort fit, weak separation triggers a continuation loop back through path evaluation, and sufficient separation closes on a single path.

entry_conditions.value: A START node initiates the procedure; no stated precondition for entry beyond reaching START.
entry_conditions.ambiguity: None stated. Whether START represents an external trigger or a standing ready-state is unspecified.

exit_conditions.value: Procedure exits at END when continuation is not justified after re-sorting.
exit_conditions.ambiguity: The criterion for 'continuation not justified' is not operationally specified in the packet.

exit_conditions.value: Procedure exits at N6 when one path is selected after re-sorting.
exit_conditions.ambiguity: N6 is a terminal node with no outgoing edges shown; whether N6 represents a distinct terminal action or simply a named exit state is ambiguous.

required_steps.value: N1: Expansion of a single state into multiple parallel paths (N1a, N1b, N1c).
required_steps.source_text: N1 -> N1a; N1 -> N1b; N1 -> N1c; N1 expands one state into multiple paths.
required_steps.ambiguity: None regarding the fan-out structure. Whether the number of paths is fixed at three or variable is unspecified.

required_steps.value: N1a, N1b, N1c: Pass-through states conveying each path to its respective N2 node.
required_steps.source_text: N1a -> N2A; N1b -> N2B; N1c -> N2C; N1a, N1b, N1c are pass-through states.
required_steps.ambiguity: Pass-through is stated but the functional role of the pass-through (labeling, tagging, identity preservation) is not specified.

required_steps.value: N2A, N2B, N2C: Parallel path-processing nodes converging at N3.
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether N2 nodes operate independently or synchronize before N3 is not stated.

required_steps.value: N3: Introduction of a differentiating input.
required_steps.source_text: N3 -> N4; N3 adds a differentiating input.
required_steps.ambiguity: The nature of the differentiating input is unspecified.

required_steps.value: N4: Re-sorting of path fit based on the differentiating input; branching decision point.
required_steps.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N4 -> END [continuation not justified]; N4 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.
required_steps.ambiguity: The sorting metric and threshold value are unspecified.

optional_steps.value: N5, N5a, N5b, N5c: Continuation loop when separation remains weak; N5 fans out again to N2 nodes via pass-through states.
optional_steps.source_text: N5 -> N5a; N5a -> N2A; N5 -> N5b; N5b -> N2B; N5 -> N5c; N5c -> N2C; N5 reopens continuation when separation remains weak.
optional_steps.ambiguity: Optional only in the sense that the loop is conditional; it is required if the threshold-not-met condition holds. Whether the loop can execute more than once is not structurally ruled out but is not confirmed.

loops.value: N4 -> N5 -> {N5a|N5b|N5c} -> {N2A|N2B|N2C} -> N3 -> N4: A re-evaluation loop triggered when separation at N4 is below threshold.
loops.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N5 -> N5a -> N2A; N5 -> N5b -> N2B; N5 -> N5c -> N2C; N2A -> N3; N2B -> N3; N2C -> N3; N3 -> N4
loops.ambiguity: The maximum number of loop iterations is unspecified. Whether all three N5 pass-through branches are always activated simultaneously or selectively is unspecified.

branches.value: N1 fans out to three parallel paths via N1a, N1b, N1c.
branches.ambiguity: Whether this is an exhaustive enumeration or a representative sample of a variable-cardinality fan-out is unspecified.

branches.value: N4 branches to N5, N6, or END based on separation outcome.
branches.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N4 -> END [continuation not justified]
branches.ambiguity: Whether exactly one of the three branches is always taken, or whether multiple could be simultaneously triggered, is not stated. Presumed mutually exclusive by procedural convention.

branches.value: N5 fans out to three pass-through branches N5a, N5b, N5c re-entering N2A, N2B, N2C respectively.
branches.ambiguity: Same cardinality ambiguity as the N1 fan-out.

termination_conditions.value: Termination via N6 when one path is selected (sufficient separation achieved).
Termination via END when continuation is not justified (no sufficient path or loop ceiling reached, inferred).
termination_conditions.ambiguity: Whether 'continuation not justified' means all paths are equally weak, a resource/iteration limit is reached, or some other stopping criterion is entirely unspecified.

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Fan-out from a single node into parallel paths, convergence through a differentiating input node, conditional re-entry loop, and a three-way terminal branch. Overall shape: diverge-converge with conditional loop and multi-exit terminal.

primitive_sequence: LOOP-REOPEN -> PASS-THROUGH (x3) -> PARALLEL-PROCESS (x3) -> CONVERGE -> DIFFERENTIATE -> SORT/EVALUATE (repeat)
primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: CONDITIONAL-LOOP with fan-out/converge structure around a sorting/evaluation node.
candidate_missing_primitives: Iteration counter or loop-limit guard (not represented in graph)
Explicit synchronization primitive at N3 convergence (not specified)
Path-scoring or ranking primitive (implied by 're-sort' but not shown as a node)

invariants.value: N1a, N1b, N1c and N5a, N5b, N5c are always pass-through; they do not modify path state.
invariants.source_text: N1a, N1b, N1c are pass-through states. N5a, N5b, N5c are pass-through states.
invariants.ambiguity: The definition of 'pass-through' (no transformation vs. no branching vs. no delay) is unstated.

invariants.value: All parallel paths (N2A, N2B, N2C) converge at N3 before evaluation at N4.
invariants.ambiguity: Whether convergence requires all three paths to complete or accepts partial convergence is unspecified.

preconditions.value: N3 requires completion of at least one (possibly all) N2 paths before the differentiating input can be added.
preconditions.source_text: N2A -> N3; N2B -> N3; N2C -> N3; N3 adds a differentiating input.
preconditions.ambiguity: Synchronization semantics at N3 are unspecified.

preconditions.value: N4 requires output from N3 to perform re-sorting.
preconditions.source_text: N3 -> N4; N4 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.
preconditions.ambiguity: None beyond the unspecified sorting metric.

postconditions.value: After N4 branching to N6, exactly one path has been selected.
postconditions.ambiguity: What 'selected' entails downstream of N6 is not shown.

postconditions.value: After N4 branching to END, no path continuation proceeds.
postconditions.ambiguity: Whether a null result, error, or default output is produced at END is unspecified.

stopping_criteria.value: Stop when one path achieves sufficient separation to be selected (N6 exit).
Stop when continuation is not justified (END exit), implying either resource exhaustion, indeterminate state, or no viable path.
stopping_criteria.ambiguity: The distinction between this and the N6 exit in near-indeterminate cases is unspecified.

validity_conditions.value: The procedure is valid only while at least one path (N2A, N2B, or N2C) remains active and reachable through the loop.
validity_conditions.ambiguity: Whether paths can be pruned or deactivated across loop iterations is unspecified.

body.note: Expands one state into multiple paths
Re-sorts path fit after differentiating input

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins at START and enters an expansion node (N1) that fans out a single incoming state into three parallel paths via pass-through nodes. Each path feeds into a parallel processing stage (N2A, N2B, N2C) that converges at a differentiation node (N3), which introduces an additional input. Control passes to an evaluation node (N4) that re-sorts path fit in light of the new input. N4 branches on three mutually exclusive (presumed) outcomes: if separation is insufficient (threshold not met), the procedure loops through N5, which re-fans out via pass-through nodes back into the N2 processing stage, re-converges at N3, and re-evaluates at N4; if one path achieves sufficient separation, the procedure closes at N6; if continuation is not justified, the procedure terminates at END.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: Single-input fan-out to parallel paths; convergence with differentiating input; conditional re-evaluation loop; three-way terminal: select-close, loop-continue, or terminate-without-selection.

ambiguities.description: Whether N3 requires all three N2 paths to complete before proceeding, or whether partial convergence is permitted.
plausible_interpretations: AND-join: all three paths must complete before N3 fires.
OR-join: any arriving path triggers N3 independently.
XOR-join: first arriving path triggers N3 and the others are discarded.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects whether the procedure is inherently sequential-within-parallel or truly concurrent. Cannot be resolved from packet.

ambiguities.description: Whether the three N4 exits are strictly mutually exclusive or could overlap (e.g., could 'continuation not justified' fire simultaneously with 'threshold not met').
plausible_interpretations: Mutually exclusive priority-ordered branch: threshold-not-met checked first, then one-path-selected, then continuation-not-justified.
Mutually exclusive simultaneous evaluation with implicit priority.
Non-exclusive: multiple exits could be triggered, with resolution unspecified.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects loop termination guarantees. Priority ordering is the most procedurally conventional reading but is not stated.

ambiguities.description: Whether the N4->N5->N2->N3->N4 loop has an implicit or explicit upper bound.
plausible_interpretations: Unbounded loop that terminates only when separation is achieved or continuation is unjustified.
Bounded loop with an unstated iteration limit, after which END is forced.
'Continuation not justified' encodes the loop-limit condition implicitly.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: If unbounded, the procedure may not terminate in all cases. Cannot be resolved from packet.

ambiguities.description: Whether N5 always fans out to all three pass-through branches or selectively reactivates a subset of paths.
plausible_interpretations: N5 always reactivates all three paths (symmetric with N1).
N5 selectively reactivates only paths that remain viable after prior evaluation.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects whether the loop narrows the candidate path set across iterations or resets it fully. Packet does not specify.

ambiguities.description: Whether N6 is a pure exit state or performs an additional action upon path selection.
plausible_interpretations: N6 is a labeled terminal state only (exit with a result).
N6 represents an additional closure action (e.g., confirmation, commitment, output generation).
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Minor effect; N6 has no outgoing edges in the packet so no structural extraction is possible either way.

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: The structural pattern of: single-input expansion into multiple candidate paths, parallel processing, convergence with an additional differentiating input, re-sorting of path fitness, a loop triggered by weak separation, and a terminal close on a single winning path is strongly consistent with a differential diagnosis or analogous narrowing-by-evidence procedure. The node note 'N4 re-sorts path fit' and 'N5 reopens continuation when separation remains weak' are structurally distinctive of iterative hypothesis narrowing under incoming evidence. Recognition was triggered by structural pattern, not domain terminology, as no domain terms appear in the packet.
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: low-moderate. Recognition is based on structural pattern matching to a well-known procedural archetype. No domain terminology was introduced into the extraction. The recognized artifact label was recorded only in the recognized_artifact field. Structural extraction was grounded exclusively in packet content. The primary leakage risk is in the AST framing and natural language summary, which use vocabulary ('candidate paths', 'separation', 'path fit') drawn from the node notes rather than introduced from prior knowledge.

confidence.structural_layer: high — graph edges and node notes are explicit; ambiguities are recorded but do not undermine the primary structural read.
confidence.primitive_layer: medium-high — primitives are recoverable from graph structure; convergence semantics and loop-bound primitives are inferred and flagged.
confidence.constraint_layer: medium — stopping criteria and validity conditions are partially inferred; operationalizations are absent.
confidence.representation_layer: medium — AST and summaries are faithful to packet content; convergence and loop-bound ambiguities prevent a fully resolved representation.
confidence.recognition_analysis: high — structural recognition basis is clear and grounded; leakage risk assessment is conservative.
confidence.overall: medium-high — packet is structurally well-defined at the graph level; semantic gaps in convergence semantics, loop bounds, and threshold operationalization limit full constraint and representation confidence.

notes: The packet is graph-only with node notes. No domain terminology appears. All structural extraction is grounded in the graph edges and node-note annotations. The five recorded ambiguities are genuine structural underdeterminations, not artifacts of extraction choices. The recognized artifact identification is consistent with the graph_only_identity_node_expanded recognition condition, suggesting the identity node (N1 expansion) was the intended recognition cue. No prior knowledge was used to fill structural gaps.
